title: Architectural invariants expansion — Python + TypeScript (index)
goal: Build a lint/test harness that autonomously steers an LLM toward idiomatic, architecturally sound code. The work is split into two lane files so two agents can run in parallel without contending on this plan file. Each lane agent reads this index for shared harness rules + its own lane file for items; writes go only to its lane file. A verifier agent re-reads each lane file and re-runs acceptance_criteria to promote needs_verification → complete.
source_conversation: Grounded in docs/references/* pack reasoning, existing apps/tests/invariants/* patterns, and Anthropic's 'Effective harnesses for long-running agents' (init.sh + progress file + per-feature JSON with passes:false + commit-per-item) — https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents.

python.summary: Import-linter layers, deptry, no-env-outside-config, no-blocking-IO-in-async, no-raw-SQL, no-print, no-asyncio.run, Ruff rule-set audit, Hypothesis properties.

typescript.summary: Protected invariants/ directory + shims + meta-invariant (XX-02), strict tsconfig, eslint-plugin-boundaries, no-restricted-imports/syntax, no-fetch-outside-api-client, no-useEffect-fetch, no-literal-route-to, custom vi.mock rule, localStorage/window ownership, top-level-singleton ban, knip, api-client drift test, TanStack Router plugin, Tailwind palette guard, no-inline-style.

parallelization_rules: The two lane files are the ONLY writable plan files. Each lane agent updates only its own lane file. This index is read-only for agents.

Code-level file surfaces are disjoint: Python lane touches apps/*/src/**, root pyproject.toml, and per-app pyproject.toml files. TypeScript lane touches web-ui/** and .claude/settings.json. New invariant test files land under apps/tests/invariants/ with distinct filenames per lane — no collision.

Cross-lane ordering constraint: XX-02 (creates web-ui/invariants/ + meta-invariant + hook globs) MUST complete before any other TS-* item starts. It is the only cross-lane gate. Two ways to satisfy it: (a) land XX-02 yourself as a setup step before spawning either lane agent; (b) run TS lane sequentially starting with XX-02 while the Python lane runs fully parallel.
No Python item depends on anything in the TypeScript lane. The Python lane can run start-to-finish in parallel with the TypeScript lane regardless of TS progress.

If you spawn lane agents via the Agent tool, use `isolation: "worktree"` so code-level edits also land in isolated checkouts. The lane-file split makes the plan JSON merge a no-op (only one file changes per lane); worktrees handle the code-level merge.
format_notes: JSON, not Markdown — per Anthropic's finding that agents are less likely to inappropriately overwrite JSON.
Each item's acceptance_criteria are executable. Verifier runs them literally; no interpretation.
status state machine: not_started → in_progress → needs_verification → complete | blocked.
Implementer sets needs_verification after its own local passes. Only the verifier agent promotes to complete.
session_log is append-only. Never rewrite history. Each entry = one session's work on this item.
Items in each lane are ordered by priority (lower number = higher priority). Burn down in order unless blocked_by says otherwise.

Every item MUST ship with a failing-test message matching the SMELL / WHY / FIX / violations shape used in apps/tests/invariants/_helpers.py:format_invariant_failure — the message is the agent-facing UX.

TypeScript rule files live in web-ui/invariants/ (HUMAN-OWNED, hook-protected), with a thin editable shim at web-ui/eslint.config.ts / web-ui/tsconfig.json. The shim re-exports / extends the protected files. A Python meta-invariant (test_webui_invariants_wired.py) asserts the wiring is intact by running `eslint --print-config` and `tsc --showConfig` against the live tree. This mirrors the apps/tests/invariants/ discipline on the Python side.

harness.bootstrap_expectation: All invariants pass on baseline (main). TS typecheck/lint may fail on baseline because web-ui skeleton is minimal — record the baseline failure set before editing so the verifier can diff.
session_startup_ritual: 2. Read your assigned lane file end-to-end (see `lanes.<lane>.file` above).
3. Run bootstrap_cmds; confirm existing invariants still pass.
4. Read git log since last session_log entry on the item you're picking up.
5. Pick the lowest-priority-number item IN YOUR LANE with status=not_started whose blocked_by are all complete.
6. Set its status=in_progress and append a session_log entry announcing intent — write ONLY to your lane file, never to this index or the other lane file.
7. Implement. Commit per item with message format 'invariant(ID): <title>'.
8. Run that item's acceptance_criteria locally. If all pass, set status=needs_verification.
9. Append a session_log entry with the commit SHA and the acceptance_criteria results.
harness.verifier_prompt: You are the verifier. Read BOTH lane files. For every item with status=needs_verification in either lane: (a) check out the repo at HEAD, (b) run every acceptance_criteria entry literally and collect pass/fail, (c) ALSO write a new deliberately-broken production-code diff that SHOULD trip the invariant and confirm it fails as expected (negative test — the single most common failure mode is an invariant that passes because it's checking nothing), (d) if both positive and negative checks pass, set status=complete and fill verifier_notes with the commit SHA verified + the exact negative-test diff used. If anything fails, set status=in_progress, leave a verifier_notes with the failure, and STOP — do not fix it yourself.
harness.commit_convention: invariant(<ID>): <one-line title>. Body: link to the lane file + the acceptance_criteria that now pass.
harness.protect_invariant_files: Python invariants inherit protection via the existing Claude Code hook on apps/tests/invariants/**. TypeScript invariants inherit protection via a new hook glob on web-ui/invariants/** (landed in XX-02). Any new invariant file created by a subsequent item MUST live inside one of these two protected directories — see `files.protect` on each item.
harness.plan_file_protection: This index file and the two lane files all live under docs/progress/. They are NOT in the hook-protected invariants directories by design — agents update status / session_log in their lane file during work. The integrity guarantee for WHAT each invariant enforces comes from the invariant code itself being hook-protected, not from the plan file.

acceptance_criteria_types.shell_exit_zero: Run cmd; must exit 0. Use for 'this test passes' / 'this linter is clean'.
acceptance_criteria_types.shell_exit_nonzero: Run cmd; must exit non-zero. Use for 'this test fails against the negative case'.
acceptance_criteria_types.grep_present: Pattern must be found in path (ripgrep regex).
acceptance_criteria_types.grep_absent: Pattern must NOT be found in path.
acceptance_criteria_types.json_path_equals: Evaluate jq path in file; must equal value.
acceptance_criteria_types.negative_test: Apply diff; run cmd; must exit non-zero. Then revert. Proves the invariant detects its target.
